overview: This fictitious catalog demonstrates how structured product information can be transformed into a professional sales document. Product names, categories, descriptions, prices, availability, and ordering notes are organized into a clean catalog layout.

categories.description: Functional items designed for daily office organization.
Compact storage products for workspaces and small offices.
Products that support scheduling, note-taking, and project planning.

products.name: Modular Desk Tray
products.description: Stackable tray for documents, notes, and daily workspace items.

products.name: Cable Organizer Set
products.description: Simple cable clips and ties for clean desk setups.

products.name: Compact File Box
products.description: Portable file box for folders, receipts, and small records.

products.name: Label Storage Bins
products.description: Set of labeled bins for organizing supplies and materials.

products.name: Weekly Planning Pad
products.description: Tear-off weekly planner for tasks, priorities, and reminders.

products.name: Project Notes Kit
products.description: Notebook and sticky note bundle for project tracking.

pricing_notes: Prices are fictitious and shown only for catalog automation demonstration.
Bulk pricing can be added as an additional structured field.
Availability status can be updated from a spreadsheet, CSV, or inventory export.
order_steps: Review product list and confirm selected SKUs.
Send purchase quantity and delivery requirements.
Confirm pricing, availability, and payment terms.
Receive final order confirmation and delivery estimate.
appendix_notes: All catalog content is fictitious and used only as a product catalog automation case study.
The same structure can be adapted for wholesale catalogs, ecommerce product sheets, price lists, or distributor brochures.
The goal is to demonstrate repeatable catalog generation without manual formatting.
